description_long: This is a stone obelisk with a rectangular shaft rising from a broad, tiered base. The shaft is plain and unadorned, with subtle weathering and discoloration visible on its surface, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The base consists of multiple square stone slabs, each layer slightly smaller than the one below, creating a stepped appearance. The stone material is rough and gray, with some darker patches and signs of moss or lichen growth. There are no visible inscriptions or decorative carvings on the shaft or base. The overall structure is solid and imposing, typical of commemorative or historical monuments found in urban parks or plazas.
description: A weathered gray stone obelisk with a plain rectangular shaft and a stepped, square base. The surface shows signs of age and exposure, with no visible inscriptions or ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad face of the shaft and the full stepped base, with some surface discoloration and weathering.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile of the shaft and the layered structure of the base, with visible seams between stone slabs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying more pronounced weathering and darker patches on the shaft and base, with the stepped design clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlighting the plain, unadorned shaft and the square, tiered base with visible stone joints.
attribute_base_design: The base is a multi-tiered, square platform composed of large stone slabs. Each tier is slightly smaller than the one below, creating a stepped effect. The surface is rough and weathered, with visible seams and some discoloration.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and unadorned, matching the rectangular cross-section of the shaft with no unique features or decorations.
